{{Quote|History is on the move, Captain. Those who cannot keep up will be left behind, to watch from a distance. And those who stand in our way will not watch at all.|Grand Admiral Thrawn to Captain Gilad Pellaeon|<ref name="TLC" />}}
The '''Thrawn campaign''', also known as the '''Thrawn crisis''', was a series of innovative military and political maneuvers executed by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and his [[Thrawn's confederation|confederation]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] in his bid to restore the fallen [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Thrawn's attacks nearly toppled the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], as well as posing as its first major internal and external conflict.

Thrawn referred to his offensive as the "'''Mount Tantiss campaign'''," since two key elements of the campaign were pieces of technology he had located inside [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] private storehouse inside [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]].<ref name="DFR" />

==Prelude==
{{Quote|I am Grand Admiral Thrawn. I have been away, but now I have returned. I know some of what has occurred. You will fill in the details of the rest when I come aboard. Rejoice, Captain, for the Empire will rise again.|Thrawn greets Gilad Pellaeon|<ref name="AGAR">{{WEGCite|story=A Grand Admiral Returns|book=Heir to the Empire Sourcebook}}</ref>}}
Following the [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|campaigns]] after Warlord [[Zsinj]]'s defeat, [[Imperial Space]] had been pushed back to a bare quarter of its former territory.<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook">''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref> Additionally, as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] lost ground, its sources of crewmen dried up. Instead of [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Academy]] graduates, Loyalist officers like Captain [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] had to make do with recruits trained on the job, and were even forced to order conscription raids to press men into service.<ref name="Heir to the Empire Sourcebook">''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref>

However, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was not in a much better state. During the relentless battles after Zsinj's death, the Empire and the New Republic had both suffered appalling fleet losses. With the [[Kuat Drive Yards Orbital Array|Kuat shipyards]] almost completely destroyed by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] itself, both sides were forced to either capture each other's ships or rely on lesser, overstretched shipyards. Given the scale of the fighting and the Empire's ever-increasing fragmentation, a speedy recovery for either was unlikely any time soon, and fleet sizes on both sides were finely balanced. Consequently, by the end of [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Mon Mothma ordered a suspension of New Republic operations in order to rebuild its forces, entrench the government and integrate its territories. Imperial Space was poorly developed economically, and she was content to let a strategy of [[Galactic economy|economics]] slowly win over systems. Thus, the Empire continued to hold its territories beyond the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]], while Zsinj's former empire between the Hydian and the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] became a region of divided loyalties known as the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]]. Meanwhile, the New Republic occupied a salient along the [[Celanon Spur/Legends|Celanon Spur]] from [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] captured during the post–Zsinj campaigns, and nominally controlled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] south of the Perlemian. However, many of these systems had yet to return representatives to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and the New Republic's authority was largely theoretical beyond the major trade routes. For a brief time, an uneasy peace existed.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref><ref name="TEA" />

In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], however, approximately two years after the capture of Coruscant, reports started to reach the New Republic of a [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] commanding the Imperial fleet. This man was [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], a [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] better known by his [[Core name/Legends|core name]], Thrawn. Returning from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], Thrawn had taken control of the Galactic Empire and started a new campaign against the New Republic.

Raising his flag on the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], Thrawn took the popular and respected Captain Gilad Pellaeon as his second-in-command. Thrawn earned the admiration of the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] through his suppression of [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] bases in Imperial territory, and warlords like [[Ardus Kaine]] and [[Delak Krennel]] agreed to a [[Thrawn's confederation|temporary military confederation]]. [[Ars Dangor/Legends|Ars Dangor's]] [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Ruling Council]] on [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] gave him the nominal position of [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], but in reality the Council and the Moffs hoarded military assets as much in fear of each other as the New Republic. The force truly available to Thrawn consisted only of the dozen Star Destroyers and their support lines still remaining in [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat at Endor]], of which only six—the ''Chimaera'', the [[Judicator/Legends|''Judicator'']], the ''[[Death's Head]]'', the [[Stormhawk (Imperial I-class)|''Stormhawk'']], the ''[[Nemesis]]'' and the [[Inexorable (Imperial I-class)|''Inexorable'']]—were judged fully loyal. Thrawn split the squadron into two fleets: [[Thrawn's fleet (Galactic Empire)|the fleet of six Loyalist Star Destroyers]] led by the ''Chimaera'' under his personal command, and a second force of six ships led by Captain [[Dorja]] aboard the [[Relentless (Dorja)|''Relentless'']]. Thrawn additionally reaffirmed the service of the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] to the Empire, using them as bodyguards and [[Noghri Death Commando|commandos]].<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

==The campaign==
===Preliminary stages===
[[File:Thrawn_yasalamir.jpg|thumb|left|[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and a [[Ysalamiri/Legends|ysalamir]]]]
Thrawn viewed his strategy as a series of interlocking pieces that added up to "the only puzzle worth solving... the complete, total, and utter destruction of the Rebellion." During this early period, Thrawn used individual Star Destroyers to raid New Republic worlds in the Borderland Regions like [[Raid on the Draukyze system|the]] [[Draukyze system/Legends|Draukyze]] [[Raid on Saarn|and]] [[Saarn system/Legends|Saarn systems]] to test their reactions. The initial phase of Thrawn's plan aimed to alleviate the problem of his limited military forces. He hoped to acquire any capital ships he could—which included stealing New Republic warships using [[Plasma-Jet Mole Miner|mole miners]], and locating and seizing the lost [[Katana fleet]]—and build an army of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] to crew his new fleet. To build the clones, Thrawn sought Palpatine's hidden supply of [[Spaarti cloning cylinder]]s, which he planned to use at an accelerated pace to fill his ranks with loyal crewmen, soldiers and pilots. But to keep the accelerated process from creating [[Clone madness|mad, unstable clones]], Thrawn planned to use the [[Ysalamiri/Legends|ysalamir]], a creature able to create a [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-blocking bubble. Thrawn learned of the creatures years earlier when he noted during his time in the Unknown Regions that [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] had been avoided by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] for the last 300 years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The ysalamiri were also crucial to Thrawn's plan to rein in [[Joruus C'baoth]], the mad Dark Jedi clone that guarded Palpatine's secret stores (though Thrawn had initially expected to find a [[Guardian of Mount Tantiss|different Guardian]]). While the Spaarti chambers would provide Thrawn with acceptable soldiers, he lacked sufficient officers to command his new fleet and determined that C'baoth could provide coordination between the Imperial forces as [[Nial Declann]] did at Endor (although Thrawn believed it was the Emperor who used [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]]).

To uncover the location of his prizes, Thrawn launched a [[Obroa-skai information raid|raid]] against the comprehensive archive computers of [[Obroa-skai/Legends|Obroa-skai]]. The raid also provided Thrawn with a wealth of up-to-date information—Thrawn's most lethal weapon—on the New Republic, its allies and the state of the galaxy at large. After the final raid, which provided Thrawn with the information he required, a [[Destruction of the Elomin task force|skirmish]] with a New Republic pursuit force afforded Thrawn his first opportunity to display his tactical brilliance by detecting the species of the pursuit force's commander—an [[Elomin/Legends|Elomin]]. He initiated an attack using a [[Marg Sabl/Legends|Marg Sabl]] maneuver that the Elomin commander would be unable to counter, and defeated the task force with a numerically inferior force.

From the partial data dump recovered from Obroa-skai, Thrawn gained the location of [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], the site of [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]], Palpatine's secret storehouse. The ''Chimaera'' ventured first to Myrkr, where Thrawn encountered [[Talon Karrde]], who was convinced to aid the Imperial commander despite his preference to remain neutral. After collecting a suitable number of ysalamiri, Pellaeon and Thrawn traveled to Wayland. Protected by [[Nutrient frame|nutrient-rack-mounted]] ysalamiri, Thrawn convinced Joruus C'baoth to aid his campaign in exchange for [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and her [[Solo twins (disambiguation)|unborn twins]]. C'baoth, enticed by the offer of young Jedi minds to shape, pledged his assistance and allowed Thrawn access to the wonders of Mount Tantiss, including the Spaarti chambers and the schematics for a working [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking shield]].

===Early strikes===
[[File:SluisVanBattle-HttE.jpg|thumb|right|Imperial fighters during the [[Battle of Sluis Van]]]]
Mount Tantiss was seeded with ysalamiri and, using exceptional warriors like [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]] and [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] as templates, the Spaarti cylinders began churning out thousands of clones, whereupon Thrawn began his military campaign in earnest. Thrawn made use of the ancient [[stateless strategy]]: he used mobility to strike at where the New Republic was not, forcing them to either overstretch their forces in an effort to defend everything, or concentrate their forces to defend a handful of important planets, and so lose legitimacy elsewhere. With the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] recently liberated from Imperial hands, Thrawn carefully aimed his attacks against a number of Inner and [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] worlds. He [[Attack on Bpfassh|feinted at Bpfassh]] in order to draw New Republic forces away from other systems, then [[Raid on Nomad City|raided]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] mining operation of [[Nomad City]] on [[Nkllon/Legends|Nkllon]], stealing fifty-one [[Plasma-Jet Mole Miner|mole miners]]. Noghri Death Commandos additionally [[Mission to Bimmisaari|raided Bimmisaari]] and [[Mission to Kashyyyk (Thrawn campaign)|Kashyyyk]] in an effort to capture Leia Organa Solo.

The feint at [[Bpfassh]] left the [[Sluis Van Shipyards|shipyards]] at [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]] poorly defended. A cloaked freighter delivered the mole miners stolen from [[Nomad City]] and their [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] escort into the system, which were used to drill through the ships' hulls and place boarding parties aboard the docked New Republic starships. Thrawn intended to steal the nearly one hundred starships, but was foiled when Lando Calrissian used the command codes he'd memorized to activate the mole miners' plasma cutters, sending them boring through the starships' command centers and disabling the ships Thrawn intended to capture. Though Thrawn did not achieve his objective, the ships were denied to the New Republic as well.

Fortunately for Thrawn, his loss at Sluis Van coincided with the arrest of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], potentially the New Republic commander most likely to provide a challenge. Unbeknownst to the New Republic, Thrawn was making use of [[Delta Source]], a [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]-era audio surveillance system installed in the [[ch'hala tree]]s in the [[Grand Corridor]] of the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]]. Not knowing the source of the leak, the New Republic government came to believe that it had a traitor in its ranks, and Ackbar was arrested at the behest of [[Borsk Fey'lya]] after Thrawn planted information implicating him in the treason.

Despite the Sluis Van setback, Thrawn's campaign was not stalled, merely delayed. In the meantime, Thrawn carried on his guerrilla strikes on Republic worlds while he built up strength, inflicting [[Taanab operation|damage]] to [[Taanab/Legends|Taanab]] and a [[Raid on Sarka|convoy]] near [[Sarka/Legends|Sarka]]. His efforts focused not on destroying Republic convoys but stripping them of defenses, forcing the New Republic to deploy more vessels to such convoys or abandon whole regions entirely, weakening them either way. Thrawn sought a new source of ships for his clone crews, seeking the [[Katana fleet]], a pre–Clone Wars force of 200 [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] that had disappeared after a [[hive virus]] had driven its crews insane. Thrawn kidnapped Captain [[Hoffner]], who had stumbled across the "Dark Force" years before, at [[Pantolomin/Legends|Pantolomin]], and his former navigator Talon Karrde to uncover the fleet's location. The New Republic raced to intercept Thrawn, but in the ensuing [[battle for the Katana fleet]], Thrawn escaped with 178 of the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaughts]], enabling him to launch his main campaign earlier than even he expected. With his burgeoning clone army and his new cloaking device, coupled with the "Dark Force," Thrawn had tipped the scales far into the Empire's hands and was poised to sweep away all resistance.

===Major offensives===
{{Quote|Economics and psychology, Captain.|Grand Admiral Thrawn|<ref name="DFR" />}}
[[File:BattleOfUkio.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Thrawn fools the planet [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]].]]
The new Imperial fleets renewed the offensive by striking up the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]] at several sectors. Thrawn's acquisition of the Katana Dreadnaughts augmented his fleet's ability to the point that it was able to immediately attack more than twenty New Republic systems.<ref name="Before the Storm">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref> Thrawn made a series of feints at [[Ando/Legends|Ando]], [[Filve]] and [[Crondre]] that forced the New Republic fleet at [[Ord Pardron/Legends|Ord Pardron]] to a sortie. Thrawn then [[Battle of Ord Pardron|destroyed]] the undefended [[Ord Pardron sector defense outpost|military base at Ord Pardron]], in turn allowing him to [[Battle at Ukio|capture]] the major agricultural world of [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]].<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

Imperial forces located in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] eagerly awaited to advanced on the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], while inner systems [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]] readied their forces to join his invasion fleets. Meanwhile, in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], warlords that heard of his campaign began to stir like fire, tempting the New Republic to deploy rapid-reaction fleets to defend Coruscant, [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], and [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]].<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

As the Republic reeled from that offensive, Thrawn committed more forces to other fronts. Further feints stripped numerous worlds of defenses, with Thrawn besieging [[Qat Chrystac/Legends|Qat Chrystac]] and [[Ketaris/Legends|Ketaris]], tying up New Republic resources. He committed to capturing [[Wistril]], destabilizing more than a dozen New Republic sectors, and he secured Imperial territory further by capturing border worlds such as Ord Mantell and [[Generis/Legends|Generis]], erasing the [[Celanon Spur/Legends|Celanon Spur]] salient. The Grand Admiral launched a [[Destruction of Nomad City|second raid on Nkllon]], giving him four months' worth of strategic resources to continue his offensive.

The New Republic fleets were crushed in individual sectors, as Thrawn's massive Dreadnaught fleet gave him a numerical advantage in each separate theatre, be it a sector-scale or planetary-scale battle. Advancing from Ord Mantell, Thrawn [[Battle of Mrisst (9 ABY)|feinted at Mrisst]] to draw the [[Coruscant Sector Fleet]] away from the capital, and launched a daring attack on Coruscant. [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] took command of the Coruscant defense, putting Thrawn, seemingly, in the position of committing himself and taking immense casualties or retreating. Demonstrating his tactical genius, Thrawn instead deployed two dozen cloaked asteroids. These asteroids blockaded the planet, and by faking signals for more than three hundred asteroids, Thrawn effectively [[Siege of Coruscant|knocked Coruscant out of the war]] while dedicating hardly any material to the effort.

The New Republic let Thrawn rampage as they hastily patched together a counteroffensive at [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]]. Hundreds of systems either declared neutrality or returned to the Empire as the New Republic's defeat seemed imminent. The since-released Admiral Ackbar pinned his hopes on retrieving a [[Crystal gravfield trap|CGT]] array from the [[Bilbringi Shipyards]], the only type of sensor that could detect cloaked objects, and so end the Siege of Coruscant. He also hoped to capture the yards and destroy the belief that Thrawn was infallible in one attack, and the New Republic assembled a fleet from all over the galaxy, withdrawing individual vessels and reshaping battle groups to provide the vessels. Ackbar feinted at [[Tangrene]] and struck at Bilbringi with the belief they would have their "second [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]" and defeat the Empire. Instead, Thrawn laid a trap and engaged the beleaguered New Republic Fleet in a decisive battle to cripple the New Republic.

===Imperial intrigues===
While Thrawn proved to gain strategic and tactical superiority with a smaller force, his armada did not represent the full strength of the Imperial remnants. The cloned [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], already consolidating power on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], arranged for a significant portion of the Imperial forces to ignore Thrawn and return to the Deep Core. It is unclear if Palpatine intended for Thrawn to be a distraction while he continued to rebuild his strength, or if his extended out-of-body period affected his mind to the point of considering Thrawn a threat rather than a loyal officer.

Writing in [[55 ABY]], [[Lenang O'Pali/Legends|Lenang O'Pali]] declared in ''[[Mitth'raw'nuruodo Reconsidered: A Patriot's Perspective]]'' his belief that Palpatine had recalled Thrawn from the Unknown Regions in order to restore the Empire to prepare to repel the upcoming [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]]. He cited Thrawn and Palpatine's discussions of the Vong (then known only as the "Far Outsiders") during their first encounter during the events surrounding the [[Outbound Flight Project]] in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], and believed that Thrawn saw the New Republic as an inefficient, corrupt government that would have to be replaced by an orderly, militarized one in order to resist the Vong. However, O'Pali's piece was highly partisan, pro-Imperial, and revisionist.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

===The Battle of Bilbringi, and defeat===
[[File:Thrawn_Dead_EC.jpg|thumb|right|The death of Thrawn]]
On the eve of complete victory, with practically all the New Republic could spare caught before him, Thrawn was defeated by a culmination of numerous factors, too numerous for even his genius to deal with at once.

On [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]], Princess Leia had unveiled the damage Thrawn had been continuing to the Noghri homeworld, rather than healing it, and they secretly turned against him, sabotaging his efforts. At the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]], Thrawn's bodyguard struck, killing the Grand Admiral. With the [[Smugglers' Alliance]] and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] coordinating, the shipyards defenses were breached, and two New Republic [[Assault Frigate Mark I|Assault frigates]] were marching on the yards. Thrawn's death, at this exact moment, meant that Captain Pellaeon had to take command, and he chose to retreat, unable to turn the tide.

On Wayland, Princess Leia, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], Talon Karrde, Luke Skywalker, Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] confronted the insane Jedi Master Joruus C'baoth, killing him and destroying the clone facilities, ruining the supply to the Empire. On [[Garos IV]], the [[Committee of Seven for Garosian Unification]] finally recovered their world, removing the source of [[Hibridium/Legends|Hibridium]] for the cloaking device technology, ruining that advantage the Empire had gained equally. Regardless, the limits to the cloaking device would prevent the Empire from reusing it again without Thrawn's genius.

With these losses, Thrawn's new Empire, ruling half of the galaxy, was left entirely rudderless, its offensive impossible to sustain, and its advantages negated in a few hours. Without Thrawn, the Empire collapsed, assisted by the clone Emperor's efforts to consolidate his own forces.

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|None of you needs to be reminded that in the past few weeks our war against the remnants of the Empire has changed from what was once called a mopping-up exercise to a battle for our very survival.|Admiral Ackbar|<ref name="TLC" />}}
[[File:Bilbringi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]]]]

The Battle of Bilbringi marked the conclusion of the Thrawn campaign. As the fallen leader's forces fell into retreat, the New Republic was allowed a brief respite before the reborn Emperor would emerge to launch another near-fatal assault. To quote Admiral Gial Ackbar's analysis of New Republic military losses:

"The New Republic sustained considerable losses in the Thrawn campaign: ten percent of our forces killed, thirty percent wounded—but those are just averages. On the worlds Thrawn actually hit, the devastation was significant."

Notably, this meant that the New Republic, which only lost a third of its territory to Thrawn, had lost forty percent of its forces—weakening its control over even more of its territory than it actually lost.

In addition, the campaign further fractured the political unity of the New Republic, giving rise to Borsk Fey'lya's political career, though Fey'lya's actions during the campaign had nearly destroyed his career. The Mid Rim resented that the New Republic had been unable to defend it during Thrawn's efforts, and New Republic forces found themselves engaged in a political quagmire after Coruscant fell to the Empire, forcing them further Rimward.

However, the campaign led to the creation of the Smugglers' Alliance, the return of Garm Bel Iblis, an increase in the organization and size of the New Republic military, the liberation of the beings of Wayland and the Noghri, and a spark of interest in Luke Skywalker to seek out other Force users that would eventually lead to his creation of the [[Jedi Praxeum]]. In the aftermath of the campaign, Ackbar led New Republic fleets back into the Outer Rim, destroying Delak Krennel's [[Ciutric Hegemony]]. However, this left the Core Worlds lightly guarded and vulnerable to a new Imperial offensive out of the Deep Core under Palpatine's command.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare" />

From the Imperial perspective, the Thrawn campaign may have been more damaging to the cause of the New Order than not. Following Thrawn's death, the disparate forces he had united fell into the [[Imperial Civil War]] after a single collaborative initiative in the retaking of Coruscant. Imperial insiders criticized Thrawn's campaign for focusing on the reclaiming of territory, but not planning on methods of holding those planets and star systems. It is unclear, however, if each of these pieces was a carefully planned segment of Palpatine's plan for reemergence, or if Thrawn did indeed have a plan that he was unable to implement before his death.

Gilad Pellaeon's role as Thrawn's second-in-command further raised his profile within the Galactic Empire, and he was further sought for that role in the years after Emperor Palpatine's final death by such warlords as [[Treuten Teradoc]] and [[Natasi Daala]]. His reputation would ultimately elevate him to [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] by [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]
